ACT ONE Scene. |

INT. SIMPSON HOUSE - LIVING ROOM - DAY

FADE IN:

The FAMILY is gathered around MAGGIE.

MARGE

Maggie, can you say ba~ba? Can you

say Ma-ma? BART

Can you say "get bent"? MARGE

Bart! BART

Mr. Rogers says it all the time. MARGE

He does not. HOMER

Maggie, can you say "daddy"? Dad-dy?

BART LISA MARGE "Eat my shorts"? "Melancholia"? “Ba-by"? "Get bent"? "Weltschmerz"? "Kit-ty"? "Go to hell"? "Zeitgeist"? "Num-num"? “Shove it"? "Je ne sais quoi"? "Bye-bye"?

The family continues to coax Maggie into talking. Maggie starts to open her mouth -- the family goes quiet.

9F08 REVISED TABLE DRAFT 5/28/92 2. Scoue oot Q

MAGGIE (BURP )

HOMER Did you hear that? She said “burlap.”

MARGE No, she didn’t. Maggie, when are you going to talk?

LISA Well, don’t push her. Remember, "Tis better to remain silent and be thought a fool than to open your mouth and remove all doubt."

ON HOMER looking perplexed.

HOMER’S BRAIN (V.0.) What does that mean? Better say something or they’1ll think you’re stupid.

HOMER Takes one to know one!

HOMER’S BRAIN (V.O.) (PROUDLY) Swish!

MARGE According to Fretful Mother magazine, if Maggie doesn’t talk by age one, we should consider a corrective tongue

extender.

9F08 REVISED TABLE DRAFT 5/28/92 3. Scoun ( tot

Marge holds up a copy of Fretful Mother magazine, featuring a woman examining her baby with a magnifying glass. The cover reads "INSIDE: IS YOUR NEWBORN FALLING BEHIND?". BART Hey, what was my first word? Marge and Homer exchange an uncomfortable look. MARGE Uh, well... DISSOLVE TO: FLASHBACK INT. SMALL APARTMENT - AFTERNOON - 1983 Scare ph Just OFF-CAMERA, we hear Marge and Homer on the bed, GIGGLING and making SEXY NOISES. Ten-month-old Bart toddles in, wearing a diaper and sucking on a bottle. BART Aye carrumba! END FLASHBACK MARGE (COVERING) You know, I don’t think I remember... HOMER (COVERING) We weren’t having sex at the time, that’s for sure. MARGE (CUTTING HIM OFF) But the story of Lisa’s first word is very cute.

BART/LISA

Tell it, Mom.

9F08 REVISED TABLE DRAFT 5/28/92 4.

Scae 2 HOMER

Yeah, tell it, Mom. MARGE Well, this story begins in that unforgettable spring of 1983: Ms. Pac-man struck a blow for women’s rights... a young Joe Piscopo taught us how to laugh... anything seemed possible in those days. Before Lisa was born, we lived in a tiny apartment on the Lower East side of Springfield. DISSOLVE TO: FLASHBACK SUPER: SPRINGFIELD 1983 Scaue 3 EXT. LOWER EAST SIDE OF SPRINGFIELD - DAY Nostalgic MUSIC PLAYS over a SWEEPING PAN of an old- fashioned ethnic neighborhood (clothesline between buildings, fruit peddlers, etc.). Immigrant urchins in Knickers and cloth caps sit on the curb. A BOY walks up to a group of KIDS. BOY Youse guys wanna play stickball? KIDS (BROOKLYN ACCENT) Soitenly. Sure. (ETc. )

They run over to a large video game, labelled SPUCKBALL™. Next to it are MUMBLETY PEG™, and KICK THE CANT,

PAN to Marge on the front steps of a tenement, holding BABY BART. She TALKS with several neighborhood LADIES.

9FO08 REVISED TABLE DRAFT 5/28/92 5. Scouse 3 ot) MARGE Did you girls watch the last episode of M*A*S*H*? IRISH LADY That Mike Farrell really boils my p’tata. ITALIAN LADY I miss-a da Klinger already. OFF-CAMERA, we HEAR Homer coming home from work. HOMER (SINGING) When the working day is done/ Girls just wanna have fu-un/ That’s all we really want... BART Homer! Bart jumps off Marge’s lap and toddles down the street to greet Homer. Homer still has some hair and weighs a trim 180 pounds. HOMER (CHUCKLING) Homer is what grown ups

call me. Call me Daddy.

BART Homer.

HOMER Daddy.

BART

Homer.

9F08 REVISED TABLE DRAFT 5/28/92 6. Sor deekd HOMER (FROWNING) Daddy. BART Da... da... da... HOMER Ye-esss... BART Domer. (LAUGHS) HOMER Why you little... Homer chokes Bart. MONTAGE of 1983 Homer, Marge and Bart doing things together. MUSIC: "GIRLS JUST WANNA HAVE FUN" 1) Homer is asleep in front of the TV. Bart climbs up on top of the TV and makes a painful swan dive onto Homer’s stomach. Homer makes a PAINED NOISE. 2) The family dines out at a neighborhood Italian restaurant. Bart is covered with spaghetti and sauce. PAN OVER to Homer, who is also covered with spaghetti and sauce. 3) Bart hangs naked, upside-down by his legs on the

clothesline. He swings around like a gymnast, five stories above the alley. Homer and Marge frantically reel him in.

INT. APARTMENT - EARLY EVENING & 4 Homer and Bart are playing in the Simpsons’ cramped studio apartment. Homer grabs Bart’s nose then sticks his thumb between his two fingers. HOMER Heh, heh. Got your nose. BART

Got your wallet.

Stan 4 ot He pulls Homer’s wallet out of his pocket. Homer runs after him, GROWLING. Bart tosses the wallet down the toilet and FLUSHES it. HOMER Nooo! Bart, what if there had been money in there? HOMER (SWEETLY) Marge that’s... Homer turns and sees Bart standing by the toilet with

MARGE

BART

Heh, heh, heh.

2) Bart leans a board against the baby, forming a crude ramp which he zips over on his Bigwheel.

Bart makes a PLEASED NOISE.

A sign reads, "STINKING FISH REALTY -- With A Name This Bad, We’ve Got To Be Good!" The logo is a dead fish with stink lines coming from it.

EXT. REALTOR’S OFFICE - DAY

INT. REALTOR’S OFFICE - DAY Marge and Homer meet with a REALTOR wearing a blazer. REALTOR Mr. and Mrs. Simpson, the only houses I have in your price range are in the neighborhood colorfully referred to as the "Rat’s Nest." INT. HOME - DAY REALTOR I think you’1l really love the colonial kitchen...

There’s a knock at the door. The Realtor answers it. A HOODLUM stands at the door.

9FO8 REVISED TABLE DRAFT 5/28/92 11. HOODLUM I got a message for Big Lou.

The hoodlum WHACKS the Realtor over the head with a crowbar. He falls to the floor in a heap.

REALTOR (IN PAIN) He’s not here. HOODLUM (MENACING) I’1l be back. EXT. ANOTHER HOUSE - ESTABLISHING INT. ANOTHER HOUSE - DAY

It looks nice. The Realtor holds a handkerchief over his nose as he talks and points out the house’s selling points.

REALTOR (MUFFLED, LIGHTLY) Now, here we have beautiful hardwood floors, track lighting...

MARGE (SNIFFS) What’s that stench?

She opens some curtains, revealing a giant "RENDERING PLANT" next door.

REALTOR Once you get used to the smell of melted hog fat, you’1l wonder how you ever did without it.

HOMER Mmmm. Hog fat.

MARGE

Let’s keep looking.

WORKER (WISEGUY VOICE, WRINKLES NOSE) Ew. Do you smell something? INT. ANOTHER HOUSE - DAY The house is on fire. REALTOR (TO HOMER AND MARGE) I’m not gonna lie to you. This one’s a fixer- upper. INT. SCeu0 g INT. GRAMPA’S HOUSE - 1983 - NIGHT. Homer and GRAMPA sit at the kitchen table. HOMER Dad, I have a problem. GRAMPA Why‘d ya’ come to me? I don’t know anything. I used to get by on my looks. Now they’re gone -- withered away like an old piece of fruit. (SOBS) HOMER

Are you done?

9F08 REVISED TABLE DRAFT 5/28/92 15. GRAMPA No, not yet. (SADLY) I was voted the handsomest boy in Albany, New York. (SNIFFLES) HOMER Dad, I don’t need advice, I need $15,000 to buy a home. GRAMPA Well, all I own is this house, (DRAMATICALLY) that I built with my own two hands. Grampa holds up his hands. HOMER You didn’t build this house. You won it on a crooked ‘50’s TV game show! GRAMPA Oh, yeah. I can still remember the routine.

Grampa pretends to be puzzled by a tough question. He mops his brow like Charles Van Doren.

GRAMPA (CONT’D) Let’s see... the atomic weight for Gadolinium... I’1l have to take a guess... one hundred fifty-seven, point three? Yes? Woo hoo!

He jumps around. Grampa and Homer share a LAUGH.

9FO8 REVISED TABLE DRAFT

GRAMPA (SWEETLY) All right, son. I’11 sell this place and write you a check. HOMER Dad, first you gave me life, now you‘ve given me a home for my family. I‘d be honored if you came to live with us. GRAMPA (QUIETLY) Thank you. They embrace. INI. SIMPSON HOUSE - THE PRESENT LISA (TEARY) What a beautiful moment. BART So, how long before you shipped Grampa off to the old folks home? ON TV

; Scere \l Cox We see an Itchy and Scratchy cartoon entitled "100-YARD GASH." CATS of many nations line up for the start of the race, with SCRATCHY representing the USA. ITCHY lowers the starter’s pistol and SHOOTS all the cats. They crawl and limp to the finish line, leaving trails of blood. Scratchy wins, and accepts the gold medal. CUT TO the White House where Scratchy, with the medal around his neck, is shaking hands with PRESIDENT REAGAN and smiling for PHOTOGRAPHERS. Reagan pulls out an axe and chops off Scratchy’s head. The reporters are shocked. Reagan pulls off his head. It’s really Itchy underneath. The reporters APPLAUD and LAUGH.

SUPER: "GOOD LUCK TO OUR ATHELETES FROM ITCHY & SCRATCHY, OFFICIAL ANIMATED CAT AND MOUSE TEAM OF THE 1984 OLYMPICS."
